     The Numbers Business Report

Hostel: Part II (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,544,812Details
International Box Office $16,061,597
Worldwide Box Office $33,606,409
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $16,828,487
Total $16,828,487
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Beth, Lorna and Whitney, three young American women traveling abroad in Rome, decide to take a weekend excursion. Lured from their intended destination by a beautiful acquaintance, the women anticipate a stay at a luxurious spa. Instead, they become pawns in a grisly game designed to entertain wealthy deviants from around the world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,203,391 (46.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.14 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$7,500,000 (worldwide box office is 4.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,350 opening theaters/2,350 max. theaters, 2.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $30,499,411

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 8th, 2007 (Wide) by Lionsgate
Video Release: October 23rd, 2007 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment, released as Hostel - Part II
MPA Rating: R for strong bloody violence and disturbing images
Running Time: 94 minutes
Franchise: Hostel
Subgenre: Slasher Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Next Entertainment, Raw Nerve, Screen Gems, International Production Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Beth, Lorna and Whitney, three young American women traveling abroad in Rome, decide to take a weekend excursion. Lured from their intended destination by a beautiful acquaintance, the women anticipate a stay at a luxurious spa. Instead, they become pawns in a grisly game designed to entertain wealthy deviants from around the world.
